summary:
Given a string made up only of the bracket characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', decide whether the string is properly balanced.

A string is valid when every opening bracket is closed by the matching type of closing bracket, brackets are closed in the correct order, and every closing bracket has a corresponding open bracket. An empty string counts as valid.

examples.explanation: Each pair opens and closes correctly with no overlap, so the string is balanced.
The brackets are interleaved rather than nested, so the closing order is wrong.

constraints: s consists only of the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']'
An empty string is considered valid
